description.p: This is a community package of Trilium Notes, maintained independently and unaffiliated with the TriliumNext project. It repackages the official upstream Linux build unmodified.

Trilium Notes is a free and open-source, cross-platform hierarchical note-taking application with a focus on building large personal knowledge bases. The desktop app is local-first: it keeps its own database and works fully offline, with optional synchronisation to a self-hosted or cloud Trilium server.

ul.li: Organise notes into an arbitrarily deep tree; a single note can be placed in multiple places at once (cloning).
Rich WYSIWYG editing with tables, images, admonitions, math and Markdown import/export, plus dedicated note types for code, canvases, mind maps and diagrams.
Attributes (labels and relations) for organising, querying and scripting notes.
Scriptable and extensible with a built-in JavaScript API and a note-map / relation-map to visualise connections.
Strong note encryption to protect sensitive notes on a per-note basis.

description.p: Permissions: this package keeps a tight sandbox by default — network (for optional server sync), display, GPU and notifications. Importing and exporting notes goes through the desktop portal, so no broad filesystem access is granted; the note database lives inside the sandbox under ~/.var/app/com.triliumnext.notes. To share a note database with a host location, grant filesystem access and set TRILIUM_DATA_DIR via flatpak override.

screenshot.caption: Hierarchical note tree and rich text editor (light theme)
Hierarchical note tree and rich text editor (dark theme)
